title: Google Cloud proposes Open Knowledge Format for agent-readable context
friendlyTitle: Google Cloud wants company knowledge to be easier for agents to read
summary: Google Cloud introduced Open Knowledge Format, a vendor-neutral specification that represents organizational knowledge as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. The format is intended to make metadata, business definitions, runbooks, and other context portable across agents and systems.
friendlySummary: Google Cloud introduced Open Knowledge Format, a simple Markdown-and-YAML way to package business context for AI systems. It is meant to make internal knowledge portable across tools.
whyItMatters: AI agents are only as useful as the context they can reliably read. A simple file-based knowledge format could help teams turn scattered docs and data definitions into something both humans and agents can maintain.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If your AI tools keep failing because they lack context, this is the kind of boring standard that can help. Better knowledge files can mean fewer hallucinated assumptions.
